title: Bitter Syrian Date Bake
description: A rich and savory dessert inspired by Syrian traditions, featuring dates, coconut milk, and a hint of bitterness.
instructions: Prepare a 9-inch round cake pan by greasing with coconut oil and lining with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, soak the pitted dates in the water for at least 30 minutes.
In a blender or food processor, combine soaked dates, coconut milk, agave syrup, lemon juice, ginger, salt, and vanilla extract. Blend until smooth.
Add the baking powder and blend for another 30 seconds.
Pour the mixture into the prepared cake pan and smooth the top.
Bake for 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
If using cardamom pods, remove them from the oven after 20 minutes and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes.
Transfer the cake to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once cooled, cut into slices and serve.
Garnish with toasted almonds or coconut flakes, if desired.
